{"key_event":"Liberal reforms and Araucanía expansion","reasoning":"Civil society strengthened noticeably through parliamentarization, expanded literate male suffrage, freer association and press, schooling, newspapers, and women’s access to university exams, though gains remained socially uneven and Mapuche autonomy was weakened. State capacity stayed very strong and edged up through Araucanía campaigns, infrastructure, codification, courts, and census knowledge, but this was offset by reduced presidential discretion and the severe fiscal squeeze after 1873.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":6.1,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":7.9}
